authoritative_source.book_text: ~/Downloads/A Philosophy of Software Design - John Ousterhout.pdf
authoritative_source.note: consolidated 2026-07-19: all 4 Ousterhout skills read ONE shared SSOT (context-library/.../philosophy-of-software-design-reference.md); per-skill copy deleted

concept_checklist: Complexity is incremental (accumulates from small decisions)
Working code isn't enough — strategic > tactical programming
Simple interface matters more than simple implementation
Comments describe what is not obvious from the code
Design for ease of reading, not ease of writing
Increments of development are abstractions, not features

fidelity.note: distillation + fixtures are COMMITTED (lightweight F2/F3 run clean without the book). book_ground_truth is an EXTERNAL licensed dependency, gitignored by design; its absence makes full-book F3 NOT-RUN (loud), never a PASS.

fidelity_status._recorded_note: RECORDED result from a run where the book WAS present (author's machine). NOT a live verdict. score.py must presence-check the book before echoing this; if the book is absent it prints RECORDED (not verifiable in this clone), never a bare PASS.

bar_provenance.F1_checklist: derived from and confirmed against the book's own design principles (source/book.norm.txt); 14/14 grounded
bar_provenance.F2_vocab: 8 named red flags confirmed present in book (subset of book's full ~14)
bar_provenance.closes: CF-068 — bar was previously authored from the proxy before the book was in hand
bar_provenance.rule: fidelity criteria are derived from the primary source, never a distillation

skill_file_provenance: vendored byte-faithful snapshot of ~/Downloads/PM-OS/.claude/skills/ousterhout-guru/SKILL.md (see skill/PROVENANCE.md). Engine does not score from it; preflight requires it present (self-containment).

expansion.principle: Deep modules — a simple interface over a powerful implementation
Errors defined out of existence — redesign so the error case can't arise
Ease of reading over ease of writing — code and comments serve the reader
Pull complexity downward — absorb it into the module, not its users

mnemonic.note: Memorable recall aid for a teaching agent; DEEP is the book's own central metaphor. Each letter must ground in the authoritative source (checked by S10) so the mnemonic is never fabricated.

signature_quotes: the best modules are those whose interfaces are much simpler than their implementations
the increments of software development should be abstractions, not features
the most important technique for achieving deep modules is information hiding
generic_blocklist: the greatest limitation in writing software is our ability to understand
this book is about one thing: complexity
voice.note: SHARP, interview-grade signature quotes only. A quote counts (S11) iff it is NOT a generic platitude (generic_blocklist) AND carries a tradeoff structure (regex in craft.py) OR a counterintuitive coined mechanism. S13 forbids declaring any platitude as a signature quote; S12 requires each verbatim in the book. All (c) Ousterhout, fair use (<15w), attributed.

retrieval.note: The loop retrieves from the authoritative SOURCE (book) first. Only if the source lacks the material does it consult the allowlist (authoritative sites). Any non-allowlisted source is REFUSED, never silently fetched. Deterministic-first; a live fallback would use WebFetch restricted to the allowlist.
